Datasheet line (as printed) & Value & Model translation & vs.\ budget \\
\hline
\multicolumn{4}{l}{\emph{Free-running FP diode} --- Thorlabs L785P090 (785 nm; Thorlabs spec sheet 21159-S01 Rev.\ E (2022))} \\[2pt]
Center wavelength (unit-to-unit) & 775--795 nm & 10 THz window & selection, not drift \\
Spectral width & not published & --- & not published \\
Mode spacing / cavity length & not published & --- & not published \\
$d\lambda/dT$, RIN, SMSR & not published & --- & not published \\
\multicolumn{4}{p{0.93\textwidth}}{\textbf{Fails by construction}: multimode with none of the budget-relevant lines published --- unusable without external characterization.} \\[2pt]
\hline
\multicolumn{4}{l}{\emph{Stabilized DFB diode} --- eagleyard Photonics EYP-DFB-0780 (780.24 nm; eagleyard datasheet Rev.\ 1.03 (2022))} \\[2pt]
Linewidth (FWHM) & typ.\ 0.6, max.\ 1 MHz & $\leq$0.5 MHz bound & $2e-05\times$ the 1\% budget \\
Temp.\ coefficient $d\lambda/dT$ & typ.\ 0.06 nm/K & 29.9 GHz/K & 1\% budget $=\pm$1.6 K \\
Current coefficient $d\lambda/dI$ & typ.\ 0.003 nm/mA & 1.50 GHz/mA & 1\% budget $=\pm$32 mA \\
SMSR & min.\ 30 dB & side-line weight 1e-03 & negligible admixture \\
RIN & not published & --- & not published \\
\multicolumn{4}{p{0.93\textwidth}}{\textbf{Spectral columns exemplary} (drift budget met by holding $\pm1.6$ K), but CW linewidth $\sim$MHz lies five orders below the bandwidth window: $P \to 0.04$ (CW limit) --- wrong class for pure heralds, ideal for center stability.} \\[2pt]
\hline
\multicolumn{4}{l}{\emph{ps mode-locked oscillator} --- Spectra-Physics Tsunami (ps) (775 nm; Tsunami datasheet (MKS/Newport))} \\[2pt]
Pulse width (sech$^2$) & $<$2--100 ps & $\sigma = 0.095$ THz & $P = 0.66$ at 10 mm \\
Repetition rate & 80 MHz & comb spacing 80 MHz & unresolved by the JSA \\
Noise (10 Hz--2 MHz) & $<$0.2\% rms & $r = 0.2\%$ & boost $-1 = 4e-06$ \\
Center wavelength stability & not published & --- & not published \\
\multicolumn{4}{p{0.93\textwidth}}{\textbf{The natural class}: drift, comb and RIN lines pass trivially; the binding line is bandwidth --- 2 ps gives $\sigma = 0.095$ THz and $P = 0.66$ at 10 mm, recovered by re-matching the crystal to $L \approx 17$ mm.} \\[2pt]
\hline\hline
\end{tabular}
